method: tie-corrected Kruskal-Wallis H on numeral magnitude (num token v) grouped by SITE within the Tablet class; site-permutation null (preserve per-site n + magnitude multiset), 5000 draws; leave-one-site-out robustness; synthetic positive control (detect + false-positive arms).
key_findings: Within the Tablet class, numeral magnitude (accounting scale) differs strongly by SITE: obs_H=45.07 vs null mean 4.09, perm_p=0.0002 (5000 draws).

The effect is GENERAL, not one-site-driven: it stays significant (perm_p=0.0002) under every single-site drop, including the magnitude-outlier Tylissos and the dominant site Haghia Triada (n=829, 79% of Tablet numerals).

Per-site medians span an order of magnitude (Phaistos 1, Khania 2, Arkhalkhori 4, Haghia Triada 5, Tylissos 10), confirming a real cross-site register on accounting scale rather than a single outlier.
Crucially, dropping Haghia Triada (which contributes 829/1047 = 79% of numerals) still yields obs_H=33.74, perm_p=0.0002 over the remaining 4 sites — the effect is not an artefact of HT's dominance.
Single-class scope (Tablet only) is honest: only the Tablet class has >=2 sites with >=30 numerals, so this is a within-class site register, not a cross-class effect.
successor_hypotheses: E067: Is the site register on accounting scale driven by a specific magnitude BAND (small counts 1-10 vs large >10), or is it uniform across the magnitude range? (per-band site test)
E068: Does the accounting-scale site register interact with document sub-structure (e.g. position of the numeral within the tablet's word stream)? (positional site test)

E069: Are the per-site magnitude distributions better described by different SHAPES (e.g. Tylissos heavy-tailed vs Phaistos concentrated at 1) than by a pure location shift? (distributional site test)
E070: Does the site register on accounting scale replicate on the non-Tablet classes if the >=30 threshold is relaxed (permutation with smaller-site pooling)? (cross-class robustness)
E071: Is the accounting-scale site register INDEPENDENT of the word-length site register found in E065, or do the two share a common site-level factor? (joint site-factor test)
E072: Within Haghia Triada alone (n=829), does accounting scale vary by sub-context (e.g. findspot/context field), decomposing the dominant site internally? (within-site sub-register)
non_circular: magnitude = corpus num token integer v (given); site = site field; class fixed = Tablet (support); null permutes SITE labels among Tablet numerals preserving per-site n and the Tablet magnitude multiset. L2 only: no sign values, no commodity identities, no reading, no metrological arithmetic. Single-class (Tablet) scope is explicit and honest.
